question:
Will Amazon dip to $148 in November?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during November 2025 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "Low" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AMZN/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AMZN/

evidences.title: AMAZON COM INC (Form: 10-Q, Received: 10/31/2025 06:02:41)
evidences.content: The increase in AWS operating income in Q3 2025, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased sales, partially offset by spending on technology infrastructure that was primarily driven by additional investments to support AWS business growth and severance costs. The increase in AWS operating income for the nine months ended September 29 Table of Contents 30, 2025, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased sales, partially offset by spending on technology infrastructure that was primarily driven by additional investments to support AWS business growth. Changes in foreign exchange rates negatively impacted operating income by $120 million for Q3 2025, and by $118 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. evidences.title: AMAZON COM INC (AMZN.MX) Q3 FY2025 earnings call transcript
evidences.content: Back to quote # AMAZON COM INC (AMZN.MX) Q3 FY2025 earnings call transcript Powered by Quartr Oct 30, 2025, 3:00 PM CST ## Earnings call Amazon delivered strong Q3 2025 results with revenue of $180.2B, up 12% YoY, and operating income of $17.4B, impacted by $4.3B in special charges. AWS re-accelerated to 20.2% YoY growth, and management raised CapEx guidance, reflecting confidence in sustained AI-driven demand. evidences.title: Amazon Q3 2025 Earnings Analysis - by Sergey
evidences.content: ### Financial Performance Revenue reached $180.2B, up 12% year over year excluding FX. Operating income was $17.4B, or $21.7B excluding the $2.5B FTC settlement and $1.8B severance costs, exceeding guidance. Trailing 12-month free cash flow was $14.8B. Net income totaled $21.2B, driven by a $9.5B gain from the Anthropic investment. Paid units grew 11%, and the third-party seller mix increased to 62%, up 200 bps from Q3 2024. Andy Jassy, Chief Executive Officer “Our focus remains on durable inputs—selection, price, and speed—so shareholders see operating leverage as we scale.” Brian Olsavsky, Chief Financial Officer “We’re prioritizing cash returns on infrastructure while maintaining flexibility to fund high-conviction AI capacity.” ### AWS [...] Compounding Your Wealth # Compounding Your Wealth # Amazon Q3 2025 Earnings Analysis ### Dive into $AMZN Amazon’s Q3 2025 earnings with review of financial performance, key metrics, operating expenses, dilution, customer growth, future outlook Sergey's avatar ### Financial Results: ↗️$180.17B rev (+13.4% YoY, +7.4% QoQ) beat est by 1.2% ↘️Operating Margin (9.7%, -1.3 PPs YoY)🟡 ↘️FCF Margin ( 0.1%, -0.5 PPs YoY)🟡 ↗️Net Margin (11.8%, +2.1...

evidences.title: Amazon Q3 earnings report: Read the release
evidences.content: Financial Guidance The following forward-looking statements reflect Amazon.com’s expectations as of October 30, 2025, and are subject to substantial uncertainty. Our results are inherently unpredictable and may be materially affected by many factors, such as fluctuations in foreign exchange rates, changes in global economic and geopolitical conditions, tariff and trade policies, and customer demand and spending (including the impact of recessionary fears), inflation, interest rates, regional labor market constraints, world events, the rate of growth of the internet, online commerce, cloud services, and new and emerging technologies, and the various factors detailed below. Fourth Quarter 2025 Guidance [...] Fourth Quarter 2025 Guidance Net sales are expected to be between $206.0 billion and $213.0 billion, or to grow between 10% and 13% compared with fourth quarter 2024. This guidance anticipates a favorable impact of approximately 190 basis points from foreign exchange rates. Operating income is expected to be between $21.0 billion and $26.0 billion, compared with $21.2 billion in fourth quarter 2024. This guidance assumes, among other things, that no additional business acquisi...

evidences.title: Amazon.com Q3 2025 Earnings Call | $AMZN | 🔴 WATCH LIVE
evidences.content: $2.5 billion for an FTC settlement and $1.8 billion for estimated severance costs. Trailing 12-month free cash flow was $14.8 billion. I'll start with AWS. AWS is growing at a pace we haven't seen since 2022, reacelerating to 20.2% year-over-year, our largest growth rate in 11 quarters. It's worth remembering that year-over-year percentage growth is a relative term. It's very different having 20% year-over-year growth on a $132 billion annualized run rate than to have a higher percentage growth rate on a meaningfully smaller annual revenue, which is the case with our competitors. Backlog grew to $200 billion by Q3 quarter end and doesn't include several unannounced new deals in October, which together are more than our total deal volume for all of Q3. AWS is gaining momentum. Customers [...] cloud services, and new and emerging technologies, and the various factors detailed in our filings at the SEC. Our guidance assumes, among other things, that we don't conclude any additional business acquisitions, restructurings, or legal settlements. It's not possible to accurately predict demand for our goods and services, and therefore our actual results could differ materially from our g...

evidences.title: Amazon Q3 2025 Earnings Preview: AWS Margins, AI Growth,
evidences.content: ### Valuation: Amazon Still Looks Reasonable At roughly $225 per share, Amazon trades at 33 times expected 2025 earnings, supported by projected EPS growth of 22% and an average annual growth rate of 19% through 2027. The company’s expected four-year revenue growth (2025–2028) sits near 11%, suggesting moderate but consistent expansion. Amazon also trades at 3.5x trailing twelve-month revenue and typically hovers around 20x cash flow , not excessive for a company of its scale. The main concern remains free cash flow, which has been volatile due to heavy investment in AI infrastructure and data centers. ### AI and the Future of AWS [...] Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) is gearing up to release its Q3 2025 financial results on Thursday, October 30, after the closing bell. Tension is in the air, and investors are anxious to see if the e-commerce titan can steady its footing after a bumpy second quarter. The spotlight is firmly on Amazon Web Services (AWS) , the company’s high-margin growth engine , as it faces intensifying competition from Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud. ### Amazon Faces Pressure After a Tough Q2 [...] ### Stock Performance: Lagging Behind the Market As of October 24, 2025...

evidences.title: Amazon (AMZN) Q3 earnings report 2025
evidences.content: ## Read more CNBC tech news For the current quarter, Amazon said it expects sales to be $206 billion to $213 billion. The midpoint of the revenue outlook, $209.5 billion, topped estimates of $208 billion, according to LSEG. Operating income is expected to be between $21 billion and $26 billion, compared with analysts' projected $23.8 billion. AI has become a major area of investment across Amazon, including in its retail, cloud, devices and ads businesses. The company is responding to an explosion of interest around generative AI, particularly after the launch of OpenAI's ChatGPT chatbot in 2022. [...] Wall Street was also looking at other key revenue numbers: Revenue in Amazon's cloud unit accelerated 20.2% during the quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of 18.1%. Cloud growth has been a key area of concern for the company, as it faces intensifying pressure from rivals Google and Microsoft, which also reported quarterly results this week. Google's cloud revenue increased 34% during the third quarter, while Microsoft Azure recorded growth of 40%. Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said in a statement that AWS is "growing at a pace we haven't seen since 2022" and touted robust artificia...

evidences.title: Amazon guidance and analyst projections discussed for upcoming periods
evidences.content: Amazon provided guidance with next-quarter revenue expected to range between $151 billion and $155.5 billion, including an anticipated $2.1 billion impact from foreign exchange headwinds. For the full fiscal year, analysts project revenue between $750 billion and $770 billion, with adjusted EPS estimated between $3.25 and $3.40.

evidences.title: AMZN Q1 Earnings Report: Date, Expectations, & History
evidences.content: ### Earnings implied volatility & options pricing Implied volatility (IV) is the expected price movement in a security for a specific forward-looking period of time. As implied volatility increases, options prices increase because the expected price range of the underlying security increases. Buyers of options benefit from increasing implied volatility, while options sellers benefit from decreasing IV. #### Earnings IV crush Implied volatility typically rises before earnings and the contracts expiring shortly after the announcement become more expensive. Immediately following the earnings report, options prices decline as volatility drops. This is known as IV crush. [...] It shows the probability of expiring in-the-money (ITM) using the stock’s HV and then calculates the probability of the stock’s price at expiration using IV. AMZN implied probability of expiring ITM #### Implied Volatility Skew Skew highlights different strike prices’ implied volatility. Generally, OTM and ITM options exhibit higher IV. We can see IV skew in these AMZN option contracts, where the OTM put options have much higher implied volatility than ATM contracts, likely because investors are looking to hedg...
